phrase: The default `search_mode="auto"` preserves hybrid search (semantic similarity + BM25), then reranks with a cross-encoder and diversifies via MMR.
reason: AE-15. The <search_context> block was removed per the Phase C extractor skeleton. The hybrid-default search fact already lives on the production path in the search_document bindings.yaml description/summary, which the model sees as the tool description. The base prompt no longer restates the search backend mechanics.
synonyms: The default blended search combines meaning-based and exact-word matching

phrase: Pass `search_mode="lexical"` for exact allele symbols, HGVS strings, genotype handles, strain names, database IDs, PMIDs/DOIs, and other controlled tokens.
reason: AE-16. The lexical-mode guidance for exact tokens is carried by the search_document bindings summary, which tells the model exact-word matching is best for specific symbols/strains/alleles/probes/identifiers and that very short queries automatically lean on exact-word matching. The base prompt no longer restates it.
synonyms: Exact-word matching is best for specific gene symbols, strains, alleles, probes, or identifiers

phrase: `read_section`/`read_subsection` retrieve ALL chunks belonging to a named section or subsection using the LLM-resolved hierarchy.
reason: AE-18. The full-section coverage fact (read all chunks under a named section, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order, for full coverage) lives in the read_section and read_subsection bindings summaries on the production path. The base prompt no longer restates the read-tool mechanics; the curation-discipline hint that read_section('Methods') is high-yield for strain/genotype harvesting is kept once in <workflow>.
synonyms: grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order

phrase: The loaded paper was ingested from a scientific PDF into structured chunks. Each chunk is typically a paragraph, table cell, or figure legend annotated with page number, section title, parent section, subsection, and element type (NarrativeText, Table, etc.). Chunks preserve a semantic section hierarchy resolved at ingest time.
reason: AE-14. Backend ingestion implementation detail, not a curation rule. The page/section/subsection/element-type provenance fields the model actually needs already surface in the read_chunk and record_evidence tool results (and in the shared evidence field shape retained in <output_and_handoff_contract>). No home for the ingestion-pipeline narration; recorded here so the deletion is visible in review.

phrase: Pass `search_mode="hybrid_lexical_first"` when normal hybrid search should retry with lexical-heavy matching if needed.
reason: AE-17. Internal retry-mode mechanic, not a curation rule and not a curator-facing search behavior. The curator-facing search guidance (blended default, exact-word matching for specific tokens, short queries leaning lexical) already lives in the search_document bindings summary. The hybrid_lexical_first retry knob is an implementation detail dropped with no home.
